Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple The Versa Director Zero Day Exploitation campaign targets water and wastewater systems using simple yet effective techniques.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Financial gain through disruption of operations

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Employees, Internet-facing assets, Web server services such as emails and databases

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2020-1054 [CWE-79]: Microsoft Exchange Server Remote Code Execution Vulnerability
stage.description: The attackers crafted a spear-phishing email targeting employees of water and wastewater systems. The email contained a malicious attachment exploiting CVE-2020-1054, allowing the attackers to execute arbitrary code on the target's machine.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2024-28995 [CWE-918]: Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type
stage.description: After gaining initial access, the attackers exploited CVE-2024-28995 to upload a malicious file onto the target's web server, enabling them to remotely execute arbitrary code.

stage.name: Impact
stage.surface: Web server services such as emails and databases
stage.description: With remote code execution capabilities, the attackers caused disruptions by manipulating hardware integrity, leading to operational disruptions within the water and wastewater systems.

Scenario.summary: The Versa Director Zero Day Exploitation campaign successfully compromised water and wastewater systems by leveraging spear-phishing and remote code execution techniques, ultimately causing operational disruptions.
